entry.title: End-to-End Warehouse and Quality Synchronization in SAP S/4HANA Using SAP EWM and QM
entry.content:
Abstract & Introduction:

The integration between SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) and SAP Quality Management (QM) plays a critical role in enabling efficient quality inspection, stock control, and warehouse execution processes within modern supply chain operations. In SAP S/4HANA environments, this integration ensures that quality inspection activities are tightly synchronized with inbound, outbound, and internal warehouse movements, thereby improving inventory accuracy, traceability, compliance, and operational efficiency.

This paper presents an end-to-end overview of SAP EWM and QM integration, covering both configuration activities and business process execution in SAP S/4HANA. The document explains how inspection lots are created during goods receipt processes, how quality inspection stock is managed within EWM-managed warehouses, and how inspection decisions influence subsequent warehouse activities such as put away, stock transfer, and outbound processing. It also discusses inspection-relevant master data, inspection types, stock postings, warehouse process integration, and inspection execution scenarios using embedded SAP EWM.

Objection of this document to show end to end integration between SAP EWM and QM, especially for Goods receipt against purchase order process. Normally consultant does not have very clarity on EWM and QM integration and people normally miss some configuration or master data and due to that some time inspection lot not even created.

Below Function module can be used if Inspection lot is wrongly created either wrong inspection type origin or associated inspection type

QPLEXT_INSPECTION_LOT_CREATE

Top of Form

Bottom of Form

Customization:

#1 Generate Inspection object type(IOT) versions:

Inspection object type controls the following:

  • inspection document creation,
  • inspection rules,
  • follow-up actions,
  • stock posting after UD,
  • warehouse inspection flow

This configuration step to create the active technical version of the inspection object type configuration. Activate and generate the runtime settings for this inspection object type so EWM QM processes can actually use it.

This is one of critical configuration, which I also missed and thought the previous version will work, but you should always do this step.

SCM Extended warehouse management-> Extended warehouse management->Cross process settings->Quality Management->Basics and Integration->Generate Inspection object type versions

#2 Maintain Inspection object type versions:

This step is used to manage the configuration version, which was created in previous version.

This is one of critical configuration, which I also missed and need to select the latest version and should not miss.

SCM Extended warehouse management-> Extended warehouse management->Cross process settings->Quality Management->Basics and Integration->Maintain Inspection object type versions

#3 Warehouse dependent IOT settings:

This setting control how a particular Inspection Object Type (IOT) behaves in a specific warehouse number.

This is also very important step, so that IOT is activated for your warehouse.

SCM Extended warehouse management-> Extended warehouse management->Cross process settings->Quality Management->Basics and Integration->Warehouse dependent IOT settings

#4 Maintain Inspection type:

An Inspection Type in SAP Quality Management defines when and how a quality inspection should be performed for a material. It controls inspection lot creation, stock posting, and usage decision processing during business transactions like goods receipt or production. I have created inspection type ZP01 by copying inspection type 1701.

Quality Management->Quality Inspection->Inspection lot creation->Maintain Inspection type

#5 Inspection type for origin

In SAP Quality Management, Inspection Origin identifies the business process that triggered the inspection lot creation. The system uses the inspection origin to determine which inspection type and quality process should be executed.

For EWM integration, inspection origin should always be 17.

Quality Management->Quality Inspection->Inspection lot creation->Inspection type for origin

#6 Default values for the inspection type

Default values of an Inspection Type in SAP Quality Management are predefined settings automatically applied during inspection processing.
These settings control behavior such as automatic inspection lot creation, posting to quality stock, and usage decision requirements.

Quality Management->Quality Inspection->Inspection lot creation-> Default values for the inspection type

#7 Define Follow-up actions for inspection results

Define Follow-Up Actions for Inspection Results in SAP Extended Warehouse Management controls what action the system should perform after Usage Decision (UD). It determines activities such as stock movement, warehouse task creation, and posting stock to unrestricted or blocked stock after inspection completion.

SCM Extended warehouse management-> Extended warehouse management->Cross process settings->Quality Management->Inspection results ->Maintain follow-up actions

#8 Code group for Usage Decision

A Code Group for Usage Decision in SAP Quality Management is a collection of decision codes used during Usage Decision processing. It groups multiple UD codes such as Accept, Reject, Rework, or Scrap under one logical category for inspection processing.

Quality Management->Quality Inspection->Inspection lot completion-> Edit code groups and codes for usage decision

#9 Selected set codes for Usage Decision:

Selected Set Codes for Usage Decision in SAP Quality Management are predefined Usage Decision codes grouped together for inspection processing.
They allow users to select standardized decisions such as Accept, Reject, Rework, or Scrap during UD execution.

As per my personal experience, I missed below configuration and I was not able to see any UD code, that has eaten significant time.

Quality Management->Quality Inspection->Inspection lot completion-> Selected set codes for Usage decision

#10 Maintain Settings at plant level

Maintain Settings at Plant Level in SAP Quality Management is used to define quality management control settings specific to a plant. It controls behavior such as inspection lot creation, usage decision processing, stock posting, and quality integration for materials within that plant.

Quality Management->Basic Settings->Maintain Settings at plant level

Master data:

#1 Material Master – QM view:

Inspection type ZP04(created above) is assigned to material) under quality view using MM01. inspection Type is assigned to the material master in SAP Quality Management so the system knows when and how quality inspection should be triggered for that specific material. It controls inspection lot creation, inspection stock posting, and quality processing during activities such as goods receipt, production, or warehouse movements. Without assigning an inspection type, SAP will not know that the material requires quality inspection.

#2 Maintain Inspection rule(/SCWM/QRSETUP):

Maintain Inspection Rule in SAP Extended Warehouse Management defines how the system should create and process inspections during warehouse activities.
It links warehouse number, inspection type, inspection object type (IOT), follow-up actions, and stock processing behaviour together.
The inspection rule controls when inspection documents are created and how stock is handled after Usage Decision (UD).

End to end Transactions:

#1 Inbound delivery creation(VL31N):

Inbound delivery is created with reference to purchase order and this should be distributed to EWM system. I am not using Handling unit(HU) in this example.

#2 Warehouse task creation(/SCWM/PRDI):

As a material receipt process, warehouse task needs to be created. And Inspection lot # 170000002613.

#3 Warehouse task confirmation (/SCWM/MON):

This step, warehouse task is confirmed.

 

 

#4 Warehouse stock(/SCWM/MON):

Below is EWM warehouse stock before quality stock

 

#5 Storage location stock(MMBE):

Below is storage location stock before quality check.

 

#6 Result recording(QA32):

User do record inspection characteristics such as measurements, defects, or test outcomes before completing the Usage Decision (UD). It helps SAP determine whether the material should be accepted, rejected, or sent for rework.

Select the Result recording

Entered the results

 

After Result recording, update UD code “A1” and then select “EWM Inspection”

 

Entered decision as “A01” and follow-up action as “EWM_A”

 

 

#7 Warehouse stock(/SCWM/MON):

After quality test, result recording and UD, EWM warehouse stock moved to F2(Un restricted)

 

#8 Storage location stock(MMBE):

Below is storage location stock after quality stock, Quality stocks has been moved to Unrestricted stock.

 

Conclusion:

The integration between SAP Extended Warehouse Management and SAP Quality Management in SAP S/4HANA provides a powerful framework for synchronizing warehouse execution and quality inspection processes within modern supply chain operations. Through proper configuration of inspection object types, inspection rules, inspection types, usage decision settings, and warehouse-dependent controls, organizations can achieve seamless quality inspection processing integrated directly with warehouse movements and stock management.

As demonstrated in this document, the end-to-end process starting from inbound delivery creation, warehouse task execution, inspection lot processing, result recording, and usage decision execution ensures accurate stock visibility, better traceability, and improved operational control across EWM-managed warehouses. A well-designed EWM-QM integration significantly improves inventory accuracy, inspection efficiency, compliance management, and overall warehouse productivity while reducing manual intervention and process inconsistencies.

 

Author: Naveen Chandra Sharma

(Naveen is SAP Solution architect having 29+ years of experience which includes 20+ years into SAP consulting with expertise in SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA and integration with other external system like MES, CIMCORP etc. He worked on various type of project flavours like Implementation, Enhancement and support projects in various industries Manufacturing, Finance, Pharmaceutical, Food, Telecommunication)

entry.title: Re: SAP Convergent Charging HA setup: load balancer desig...
entry.content:
Hello

Find below few answers to your questions

  • In an SAP CC HA landscape with 3 dispatchers, 3 raters, 3 guiders, 3 bulkloaders and 2 updaters, how should the external load balancer be configured for updater Web Services?
    --> it should be configure in failover method. Meaning using first Updater by default and move to the second in case of failure.

  • Since the SAP CC updater has no real load balancing and only one updater should be active, should the load balancer target group contain only the currently active updater?
    --> see answer to question 1

  • Should the standby updater be completely excluded from the active load balancer target group until failover, or should it be included with a health check that understands the standby state?
    --> the second updater will be responsive as soon as the first one is off (not before). Checking is state when the first one is up is useless.

  • What is the recommended health check for updater Web Services? --> see the documentation "SOAP Connection Check"

  • If the static URL of the active updater is used and the updater still fails under load, what should be checked first?
    --> You have to the check the java GC behavior, the number of WS threads, the DB latency and the OS CPU. As all these points could be responsible of performance issue, you cannot prioritize and you have to check each items

  • Which Admin+ commands, indicators, traces or logs should be collected to prove whether the failure is caused by:
    --> see the SAP note 2667659 - Data to retrieve for a first level analysis of SAP Convergent Charging platform.

Best regards,

Eric F.

entry.title: Re: Unable to fix plant wise profit center and Cost cente...
entry.content:
For  small price difference GL/ round off GL  in MIRO 

The automatic account assignment determination need to be defined in the highest level in Configuration Activity / SSCUI "Manage Default Account Assignments".

See kba 3322449 Account assignment errors when using detailed level

See also  3750765 - Balancing field "&1" is not filled in line item &2 raised for Supplier Invoice with minor differences

entry.title: Integrating Joule with SAP SuccessFactor
entry.content:
As organizations accelerate their digital HR transformation, the demand for intelligent, intuitive, and employee-centric solutions has never been higher. SAP Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot, brings a new layer of intelligence to SAP SuccessFactors by enabling natural language interactions, real-time insights, and guided decision-making across HR processes. Together, Joule and SuccessFactors redefine how HR teams and employees engage with talent data—shifting from transactional workflows to proactive, insight-driven experiences.

When integrated with SAP SuccessFactors, Joule acts as a smart HR assistant embedded directly into HR workflows. SAP Joule with SAP SuccessFactors transforms the way organizations manage and experience HR by combining intelligent automation with contextual, AI-driven insights. From enabling natural language queries and guided HR actions to delivering real-time workforce intelligence and seamless employee self-service, Joule simplifies complex HR processes across the organization. These capabilities not only empower HR teams and leaders to make faster, data-backed decisions but also reduce administrative workload, accelerate access to insights for managers, and enhance the overall employee experience through conversational HR. As a result, organizations achieve stronger adoption of SAP SuccessFactors features while unlocking greater strategic value from their HR technology investment.

Here, we outline the key steps involved in integrating SAP Joule with SAP SuccessFactors to enable intelligent, AI-driven HR experiences across your organization.

Pre-requisites:

  • SAP BTP Subaccount with Joule(das-application) Check the entitlements in your SAP BTP Global Account.
  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition / SAP Start.
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services which usually will be delivered with SF tenant.

Roles required to configure:

  • SAP BTP Global Account Admin & BTP Subaccount Admin (in case they are different users)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Administrator
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services Administrator

Procedure:

  • Activating Joule and Running the Booster
  • Establish Trust with your Cloud Identity, BTP and Create Trusted Domains.
  • Activating Joule in SF.
  • Activate Cloud Foundry, Create Destinations and Work zone Content Provider.
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Source & Target Configurations

License:

Joule is included as part of your SAP SuccessFactors license at no additional cost with a certain number of free messages for an annual period known as Base AI. Some of the AI Capabilities are part of the premium edition - Premium AI, where you may have to purchase AI Units to use the functionalities.

  • SuccessFactors customers will receive a Joule message allocation based on the number of licensed, active users; Users licensed for multiple products only count once.
  • Messages will be metered on an annual basis; at the end of each term, the annual allocation of free messages will reset (i.e., no carryover)
  • Customers must purchase additional SAP AI Units (Premium AI) if they exceed their free message allocation during the annual period
  • In case the service is not visible in your BTP Cockpit; to gain access to Joule, all customers will need to license a no-cost SAP SKU. Doing so will trigger the provisioning of Joule and the creation of the terms and conditions that must be accepted by the customer to use Joule.

Architecture Diagram:

 

Configuration Steps:

  • In BTP cockpit check if the Sucessfactor System is connected to BTP System Landscape and registered. If the system is not connected, we need to manually add the system to the landscape by registering the token successfully with SF.
  • Create a New subaccount in BTP cockpit for SF Joule.
  • Establish trust between the IAS and the BTP cockpit in the subaccount trusted configuration by adding the IAS URL.
  • Add the SF URL to the trusted domain in both BTP and IAS.
  • Create SAP Build Work zone service in the SF subaccount.
  • Run the Joule Booster.

 

Select the respective IAS, Build workzone and the SF tenant and finish the Booster run.

 

  • Login to SF with admin user and create a group, add the enable joule role to this group and assign to the users for enabling Joule icon. This group is required to be added to the users who are accessing the Joule to get the Joule Icon for them.

 

  • Enable cloud foundry and create a service key
  • A new role “Launchpad_Admin” will be created in BTP cockpit after creating the service key. Assign it to the configuration user before performing next steps.
  • The next step is to create destinations. Destinations LPS_SFSF_rt and SFSF_SSO are already created when running the booster.
  • Create a new destination named Navigation service. Open the service key and copy the portal service, client id, client secret and URL and paste in the destinations.
  • Create second destination named LPS_SFSFS_dt. For URL please follow the note 2215682.
  • In Build work zone URL and create a new content provider and give the design time and runtime destinations and check the report which will give the assigned roles imported to the content provider.

 

 

  • Login to IAS and check the sf.api.version for SF source system is 2. If it is 1 then we need to get the Json files from SAP portal for source and target systems and import it. For version 2 no action needed

 

Login to IAS and Import the downloaded Source and target file.

Then Download the certificate from IAS in source system and Import in SF.

 

 

  • Similarly Import the target file to IAS.
  • Finally run the read job in IAS to sync the configuration. We can see the logs from provisioning logs once it is completed. After the job is completed, we can see the roles assigned to specific users in work zone

Conclusion:

This completes the configuration of Joule for SF. Users can ask for SF queries to Joule and Joule will respond back. Please go through the blog and provide your valuable feedback and feel free to reach out to me in comment section for any doubts.
